title: Understanding Angular Momentum
text: Angular momentum (L) is given by the formula $$L=I \omega$$, where I is the moment of inertia and ω is the angular velocity.

title: Moment of Inertia for a Rod
text: For a rod rotating about its center, the moment of inertia (I) is given by $$I=\frac{1}{12} m L^2$$, where $$m$$ is the mass and L is the length of the rod.

title: Calculating Angular Velocity
text: Angular velocity (ω) can be calculated using $$ω=2\pi f$$, where f is the frequency in revolutions per second.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the moment of inertia (I) for the propeller using the formula $$I=\frac{1}{12} m L^2$$. Remember to consider the mass and length of both blades.

title: Determine Angular Velocity at $$t=10$$ s
text: First, find the angular velocity at $$t=10$$ s. The propeller reaches $$1200$$ rpm in $$30$$ seconds. Use the formula $$ω=2\pi f$$, where f is the frequency in revolutions per second.

title: Calculate Angular Momentum at $$t=10$$ s
text: Now, use the formula $$L=I \omega$$ to find the angular momentum at $$t=10$$ s. Use the moment of inertia and angular velocity you calculated.
